title: Jim Morita
content:

''[[Captain America: The First Avenger Adaptation]]'' |actor = [[Kenneth Choi]] |status = Deceased}} {{Quote|Guten Tag,Translates from German to: "''Good day!''" boys. All right, nice and calm. No sudden moves, or we'll tie a blasting cap to your... Hey, [[Dum Dum Dugan|Dugan]], what's the German word for nuts?|Jim Morita|Shadows}} Private '''James "Jim" Morita''' was a [[United States Army]] soldier during the [[World War II]] and a member of the [[Howling Commandos]]. After being rescued from a [[Hydra]] prison camp, he was recruited into the newly formed Howling Commandos by [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]] and [[Bucky Barnes]]. ==Biography== {{MD|James Morita (Earth-199999)}} ===World War II=== Jim Morita was a Japanese-American soldier who was born in [[Fresno]], [[California]]. Originally, he served in the US Army Nisei Squadron as a Ranger under the command of [[Happy Sam Sawyer]]. However, by [[1943|October 1943]], Morita had been captured by [[Hydra]] and sent to the [[Hydra Base Camp C3|weapons facility]] in [[Austria]].{{Ref|CATFA}} ====Rescue Mission==== {{Quote|What, are we taking everybody?"
"I'm from [[Fresno]], Ace.|[[Dum Dum Dugan]] and Jim Morita|Captain America: The First Avenger}} [[File:Commando Recruitment 1.png|{{AltFloat}}|Morita agrees to join the [[Howling Commandos]]]] In [[1943|November 1943]], [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]] infiltrated the facility and [[Liberation of Allied Prisoners of War|liberated]] the prisoners. Morita and his companions broke free with Captain America's help, although he then made sure to tell [[Dum Dum Dugan]] after the latter implied that he may have been one of the Imperial Japanese members and thus one of the Axis soldiers that he was actually from [[Fresno]], [[California]], and thus American and of the Allied Powers, also showing his dog tags to prove it. He and the others then wreaked havoc on the [[Hydra]] soldiers, with Morita teaming up with [[Jacques Dernier]] who used a gun powered by the [[Tesseract]] to destroy parts of the base. Morita joined the rest of the freed soldiers and returned to the Allied military base in [[Italy]] immediately after. [[File:Gabe Morita.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|[[Gabe Jones]] and Morita intercept a [[Hydra]] radio signal]] Shortly after their break-out, Morita and his comrades were invited to join a [[Howling Commandos|special elite squadron]] led by Rogers himself. Morita accepted, despite the perspective of coming back to the front-line immediately after merely escaping with his life. Along with the other Howlers, Morita took part in every assault against Hydra, he was able to intercept a [[Hydra]] radio message allowing them to locate [[Arnim Zola]]. Captain America, [[Bucky Barnes]] and [[Gabe Jones]] attacked the train but only Rogers and Jones [[Capture of Arnim Zola|returned with Zola]]. During the attack on [[Johann Schmidt]]'s fortress, Morita and the other [[Howling Commandos]] were the first to attack. Morita called the rest of the army unit to attack and they were able to capture the fortress. Morita was put in charge of the radios and was able to receive a call from Rogers. When it became clear that Rogers intended to crash the plane to prevent it from reaching [[United States of America|America]], [[Chester Phillips]] told Morita to leave the room so only [[Peggy Carter]] could listen. Rogers crashed the plane and went missing in action.{{Ref|CATFA}} ====Capturing Werner Reinhardt==== [[File:Shadows promo 3.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Morita recovers the [[Obelisk]]]] {{Quote|These things can't simply be boxed up. Someone needs to establish a permanent unit during peacetime."
"To keep dangerous technology contained."
"And watch over people like [[Howard Stark|Stark]] who are toying with it.|[[Peggy Carter]] and Jim Morita|Shadows}} Morita, along with [[Strategic Scientific Reserve|SSR]] agent [[Peggy Carter]] and fellow commando [[Dum Dum Dugan]], then captured the last known [[Hydra Research Facility Number 4|Hydra base]] in [[Austria]], along with Hydra General [[Werner Reinhardt]] and an artifact of unknown origin known as the [[Obelisk]] due to its shape.{{Ref|AoS201}} ===After the War=== Morita had a child after the war, who would eventually give him a [[Morita|grandson]] resembling him.{{Ref|SMH}} He would eventually pass away, and [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] [[S.H.I.E.L.D. Files|files]] given to [[Steve Rogers]] when he awoke in the 21st century reported Morita's status as deceased.{{Ref|TADS}} ==Abilities== *'''Expert Marksman''': Morita's training with the [[United States Army]] gave him great ability in firearm usage, being able to kill multiple [[Hydra]] soldiers in a small span of time with relative ease. *'''Bilingualism''': Morita was fluent in his native Japanese, as well as English.[[:File:S.H.I.E.L.D. personell files 2.jpg|S.H.I.E.L.D. Files]] ==Equipment== ===Weapons=== *'''{{WPS|M3 submachine gun|M3A1 "Grease Gun"}}''': [[File:Ww22.png|thumb|250px|Morita raids [[Hydra Research Facility Number 4]]]] Morita carried this submachine gun as his weapon of choice, and he used it during the [[Howling Commandos]]' raids on [[Hydra]] facilities and during the [[Attack on Hydra Headquarters]]. He continued to use it as he worked together with Agent [[Peggy Carter]] to raid the [[Hydra Research Facility Number 4]] and imprison [[Werner Reinhardt]]. ==Facilities== *'''[[Whip & Fiddle]]''': After being [[Liberation of Allied Prisoners of War|liberated]], Morita went to the pub to drink. There, he was asked by [[Steve Rogers]] to form a [[Howling Commandos|team]], which he gladly accepted. *'''[[Strategic Scientific Reserve Headquarters]]''': Morita helped his [[Howling Commandos|team]] make plans for how to tackle each [[Hydra]] base. They did so on a giant map in the middle of the headquarters. ===Prisons=== *'''[[Hydra Base Camp C3]]''': Morita was taken prisoner by [[Hydra]] and held in their base camp C3 until he was brokenout by [[Steve Rogers]]. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *[[Tadashi Morita]] - Father *[[Emiko Morita]] - Mother *[[William Morita]] - Brother *[[Morita]] - Grandson ===Allies=== *[[United States Army]] *[[Howling Commandos]] **[[Steve Rogers|Steve Rogers/Captain America]] - Former Leader **[[Bucky Barnes]] **[[Dum Dum Dugan]] **[[Gabe Jones]] **[[James Montgomery Falsworth]] † **[[Jacques Dernier]] † **[[Happy Sam Sawyer]] - Former Superior *[[Strategic Scientific Reserve]] **[[Chester Phillips]] † - Superior **[[Peggy Carter]] † {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Hydra]] **[[Red Skull|Johann Schmidt/Red Skull]] **[[Arnim Zola]] † **[[Werner Reinhardt]] † {{Col-end}} ==Appearances== In chronological order: *{{Appearance|CAFV7}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|CATFA}} **{{Appearance|CATFAA1}} **{{Appearance|CATFAA2}} **{{Appearance|CAFV6}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|CAFV8}} (concurrent events) *{{Appearance|AoS201}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|CATWS}} (photo) *{{Appearance|AoS211}} (photo) *{{Appearance|SMH}} (photo) *{{Appearance|DW}} (footage) ==Trivia== *In the comics, Jim Morita was a Nisei soldier who assisted [[Nick Fury]] on several occasions with his team, and got rescued from a Nazi prison camp by the [[Howling Commandos]] and soon joined the team. *Morita is 5'8 and weighs 155 lbs. ==References== {{Gallery|Jim Morita/Gallery}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel}} *{{WP}} [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Captain America: The First Avenger Characters]] [[Category:Agents of S.H.I.E.L.D. Characters]] [[Category:Comic Characters]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Black Hair]] [[Category:Gray Hair]] [[Category:Brown Eyes]] [[Category:United States Army Soldiers]] [[Category:Hydra Base Camp C3 Inmates]] [[Category:Howling Commandos Members]] [[Category:Strategic Scientific Reserve Soldiers]] [[Category:High Body Count]] [[Category:Deceased Characters]]
